author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XLIV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm is addressed “to the sons of Korah,” as its title shows.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now Korah is equivalent to the word baldness; and we find in the Gospel that our Lord Jesus Christ was crucified in “the place of a skull.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is clear then that this Psalm is sung to the “sons of His ‘Passion.’”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now we have on this point a most certain and most evident testimony from the Apostle Paul; because that at the time when the Church was suffering under the persecutions of the Gentiles, he quoted from hence a verse, to insert by way of consolation, and encouragement to patience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that which he inserted in his Epistle, is said here: “For Thy sake are we killed all the day long; we are counted as sheep for the slaughter.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us then hear in this Psalm the voice of the Martyrs; and see how good is the cause which the voice of the Martyrs pleads, saying, For Thy sake, etc.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title then is not simply “To the sons of Korah,” but, “For understanding, to the sons of Korah.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the case also with that Psalm, the first verse of which the Lord Himself uttered on the Cross: “My God, My God, look upon Me; why hast Thou forsaken Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “transferring us in a figure” to what He was saying, and to His own Body (for we are also “His Body,” and He is our “Head”), He uttered from the Cross not His own cry, but ours.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God never “forsook” Him: nor did He Himself ever depart from the Father; but it was in behalf of us that He spake this: “My God, My God, why hast thou forsaken Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there follows, “Far from My health are the words of My offences:” and it shows in whose person He said this; for sin could not be found in Him.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, we have heard with our ears; our fathers have told us the work that Thou didst in their days, and in the days of old” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wondering wherefore, in these days, He has seemingly forsaken those whom it was His will to exercise in sufferings, they recall the past events which they have heard of from their fathers; as if they said, It is not of these things that we suffer, that our fathers told us!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in that other Psalm also, He said this, “Our fathers trusted in Thee; they trusted, and Thou didst deliver them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I am a worm and no man; a reproach of men, and the outcast of the people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They trusted, and Thou didst deliver them; have I then hoped, and hast Thou forsaken me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And have I believed upon Thee in vain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And is it in vain that my name has been written in Thy Book, and Thy name has been inscribed on me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What our fathers told us was this:

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy hand destroyed the nations; and Thou plantedst them: Thou didst weaken the peoples, and cast them out” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is to say: “Thou didst drive out ‘the peoples’ from their own land, that Thou mightest bring ‘them’ in, and plant them; and mightest by Thy mercy stablish their kingdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the things that we heard from our fathers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps it was because they were brave, were men of battle, were invincible, were well-disciplined, and warlike, that they could do these things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Far from it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is not what our fathers told us; this is not what is contained in Scripture.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what does it say, but what follows?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For they gat not the land in possession by their own sword, neither did their own arm save them; but Thy right hand, and Thine arm, and the light of Thy countenance” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy “right hand” is Thy Power: Thine “arm” is Thy Son Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And “the light of Thy countenance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What means this, but that Thou wert present with them, in miracles of such a sort that Thy presence was perceived.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when God’s presence with us appears by any miracle, do we see His face with our own eyes?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No. It is by the effect of the miracle He intimates to man His presence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In fact, what do all persons say, who express wonder at facts of this description?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I saw God present.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But Thy right hand, and Thine arm, and the light of Thy countenance; because Thou pleasedst in them:” i.e. didst so deal with them, that Thou wert well-pleasing in them: that whoso considered how they were being dealt with, might say, that “God is with them of a truth;” and it is God that moves them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was He then other than now He is?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Away with the supposition.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what follows?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art Thyself my King and my God.” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art Thyself;” for Thou art not changed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I see that the times are changed; but the Creator of times is unchanged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art Thyself my King and my God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art wont to guide me: to govern me, to save me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou who commandest salvation unto Jacob.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Thou who commandest”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even though in Thine own proper Substance and Nature, in which Thou art whatsoever Thou art, Thou wast hid from them; and though Thou didst not converse with the fathers in that which Thou art in Thyself, so that they could see Thee “face to face,” yet by any created being whatsoever “Thou commandest salvation unto Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that sight of Thee “face to face” is reserved for those set free in the Resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the very “fathers” of the New Testament too, although they saw Thy mysteries revealed, although they preached the secret things so revealed to them, nevertheless said that they themselves saw but “in a glass, darkly,” but that “seeing face to face” is reserved to a future time, when what the Apostle himself speaks of shall have come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When Christ our life shall appear, then shall ye also appear with Him in glory.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is against that time then that vision “face to face” is reserved for you, of which John also speaks: “Beloved, we are now the sons of God: and it doth not yet appear what we shall be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know that, when He shall appear, we shall be like Him; for we shall see Him as He is.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although then at that time our fathers saw Thee not as Thou art, “face to face,” although that vision is reserved against the resurrection, yet, even though they were Angels who presented themselves, it is Thou, “Who commandest salvation unto Jacob.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art not only present by Thine own Self; but by whatsoever created being Thou didst appear, it is Thou that dost “command” by them, that which Thou doest by Thine own Self in order to the salvation of Thy servants: but that which they do whom Thou “commandest” it, is done to procure the salvation of Thy servants.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then Thou art Thyself “my King and my God, and Thou commandest salvation unto Jacob,” wherefore are we suffering these things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps it is only what is past that has been described to us: but nothing of the kind is to be hoped for by us for the future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay indeed, it is still to be hoped for.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Through Thee will we winnow away our enemies” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our fathers then have declared to us a work that Thou didst “in their days, and in the days of old,” that Thy hand destroyed the Gentiles: that Thou “didst cast out the peoples; and didst plant them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such was the past; but what is to be hereafter?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Through Thee we shall winnow away our enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A time will come, when all the enemies of Christians will be winnowed away like chaff, be blown like dust, and be cast off from the earth.…Thus much of the future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will not trust in my bow,” even as our fathers did not in “their sword.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Neither shall my sword help me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou hast saved us from our enemies” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This too is spoken of the future under the figure of the past.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this is the reason that it is spoken of as if it were past, that it is as certain as if it were past.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Give heed, wherefore many things are expressed by the Prophets as if they were past; whereas it is things future, not past facts that are the subject of prophecy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the future Passion of our Lord Himself was foretold: and yet it says, “They pierced My hands and My feet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They told all My bones;” not, “They shall pierce,” and “shall tell.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They looked and stared upon Me;” not “They shall look and stare upon Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They parted My garments among them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It does not say, “They shall part” them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these things are expressed as if they were past, although they were yet to come: because to God things to come also are as certain as if they were past.…It is for this reason, in consequence of their certainty, that those things which are yet future, are spoken of as if past.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This it is then that we hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is, “Thou hast saved us from our enemies, and hast put them to shame that hated us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In God will we boast all the day long” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe how he intermingles words expressive of a future time, that you may perceive that what was spoken of before as in past time was foretold of future times.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In God will we boast all day long; and in Thy name will we confess for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “We shall boast”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What, “We shall confess”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Thou hast “saved us from our enemies;” that Thou art to give us an everlasting kingdom: that in us are to be fulfilled the words, “Blessed are they that dwell in Thine house: they will be always praising Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then we have the certainty that these things are to be hereafter, and since we have heard from our fathers that those we spoke of were in time past, what is our state at present?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But now Thou hast cast us off, and put us to shame” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast “put us to shame” not before our own consciences, but in the sight of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there was a time when Christians were persecuted; when in every place they were outcasts, when in every place it used to be said, “He is a Christian!” as if it conveyed an insult and reproach.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where then is He, “our God, our King,” who “commands salvation unto Jacob”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is He who did all those works, which “our fathers have told us”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is He who is hereafter to do all those things which He revealed unto us by His Spirit?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is He changed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No. These things are done in order to “understanding, for the sons of Korah.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we ought to “understand” something of the reason, why He has willed we should suffer all these things in the mean time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “all things”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But now Thou hast cast us off and put us to shame: and goest not forth, O God, in our powers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We go forth to meet our enemies, and Thou goest not forth with us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We see them: they are very strong, and we are without strength.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is that might of Thine?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where Thy “right hand,” and Thy power?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where the sea dried up, and the Egyptian pursuers overwhelmed with the waves?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where Amalek’s resistance subdued by the sign of the Cross?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thou, O God, goest not forth in our powers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast turned us away backward in presence of our enemies” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 10), so that they are, as it were, before; we, behind; they are counted as conquerors, we as conquered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And they which hate us spoiled for themselves.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What did they “spoil” but ourselves?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou has given us like sheep appointed for meat, and hast scattered us among the nations” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have been “devoured” by “the nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those persons are meant, who, through their sufferings, have by process of assimilation, becomes part of the “body” of the Gentile world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Church mourns over them, as over members of her body, that have been devoured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast sold Thy people for no price” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we see whom Thou hast made over; what Thou hast received, we have not seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And there was no multitude in their jubilees.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when the Christians were flying before the pursuit of enemies, who were idolaters, were there then held any congregations and “jubilees” to the honour of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were those Hymns chanted in concert from the Churches of God, that are wont to be sung in concert in time of peace, and to be sounded in a sweet accord of the brotherhood in the ears of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou madest us a reproach to our neighbours; a scorn and a derision to them that are round about us” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou madest us a similitude among the heathen” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by a “similitude”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is when men in imprecating a curse make a “similitude” of his name whom they detest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “So mayest thou die;” “So mayest thou be punished!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What a number of such reproaches were then uttered!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “So mayest thou be crucified!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even in the present day there are not wanting enemies of Christ (those very Jews themselves), against whom whensoever we defend Christ, they say unto us, “So mayest thou die as He did.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they would not have inflicted that kind of death had they not an intense horror of dying by such a death: or had they been able to comprehend what mystery was contained in it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the ointment is applied to the eyes of the blind man, he does not see the eye-salve in the physician’s hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the very Cross was made for the benefit even of the persecutors themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hereby they were healed afterwards; and they believed in Him whom they themselves had slain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou madest us a similitude among the heathen; a shaking of the head among the peoples,” a “shaking of the head” by way of insult.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They spake with their lips, they shook the head.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This they did to the Lord: this to all His Saints also, whom they were able to pursue, to lay hold of, to mock, to betray, to afflict, and to slay.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My shame is continually before me; and the confusion of my face has covered me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the voice of him that reproacheth and blasphemeth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 16): that is to say, from the voice of them that insult over me, and who make it a charge against me that I worship Thee, that I confess Thee! and who make it a charge against me that I bear that name by which all charges against me shall be blotted out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the voice of him that reproacheth and blasphemeth,” that is, of him that speaketh against me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “By reason of the enemy and the persecutor.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is the “understanding” conveyed here?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those things which are told us of the time past, will not be done in our case: those which are hoped for, as to be hereafter, are not as yet manifest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those which are past, as the leading out of Thy people with great glory from Egypt; its deliverance from its persecutors; the guiding of it through the nations, the placing of it in the kingdom, whence the nations had been expelled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are those to be hereafter?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The leading of the people out of this Egypt of the world, when Christ, our “leader” shall appear in His glory: the placing of the Saints at His right hand; of the wicked at His left; the condemnation of the wicked with the devil to eternal punishment; the receiving of a kingdom from Christ with the Saints to last for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the things that are yet to be: the former are what are past.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the interval, what is to be our lot?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Tribulations!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That it may be seen with respect to the soul that worships God, to what extent it worships God; that it may be seen whether it worships Him “freely” from whom it received salvation “freely.”…What hast thou given unto God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wert wicked, and thou wert redeemed!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hast thou given unto God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is there that thou hast not “received” from Him “freely”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason is it named “grace,” because it is bestowed (gratis, i.e.) freely.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is required of thee then is this, “that thou too shouldest worship “Him freely;” not because He gives thee things temporal, but because He holds out to thee things eternal.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All this is come upon us; yet have we not forgotten Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by, “have not forgotten Thee”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Neither have we behaved ourselves frowardly in Thy covenant.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Our heart has not turned back; and Thou hast turned aside our goings out of Thy way” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See here is “understanding,” in that “our heart has not gone back;” that we have not “forgotten Thee, have not behaved frowardly in Thy covenant;” placed as we are in great tribulations, and persecutions of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast turned aside our goings out of Thy way.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our “goings” were in the pleasures of the world; our “goings” were in the midst of temporal prosperities.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast taken “our goings out of Thy way;” and hast shown us how “strait and narrow is the way that leadeth unto life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by, “hast turned aside our goings out of Thy way”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is as if He said, “Ye are placed in the midst of tribulation; ye are suffering many things; ye have already lost many things that ye loved in this life: but I have not abandoned you on the way, the narrow way that I am teaching you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye were seeking “broad ways.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do I tell you?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the way we go to everlasting life; by the way ye wish to walk, ye are going to death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How “broad and wide is the road that leads to destruction: and” how “many there be that find it!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How strait and narrow the way that leadeth unto life, and” how “few there be” that walk therein!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the few?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who patiently endure tribulations, patiently endure temptations; who in all these troubles do not “fall away:” who do not rejoice in the word “for a season” only; and in the time of tribulation fade away, as on the sun’s arising; but who have the “root” of “love,” according to what we have lately heard read in the Gospel.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou hast brought us low in the place of infirmity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 18): therefore Thou wilt exalt us in the place of strength.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the shadow of death has covered us” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this mortality of ours is but the “shadow” of death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The true death is condemnation with the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If we have forgotten the Name of our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here is the “understanding” of the “sons of Korah.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And stretched out our hands to a strange God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Shall not God search this out?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He knoweth the secrets of the heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He “knows,” and yet He “searches them out”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He knows the secrets of the heart, what do the words, “Shall not God search it out,” do there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He “knows” it in Himself; He “searches it out” for our sakes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is for this reason God sometimes “searches a thing out;” and speaks of that becoming known to Himself, which He is Himself making known to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is speaking of His own work, not of His knowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We commonly say, “A gladsome day,” when it is fine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet is it the day itself that experiences delight?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No: we speak of the day as gladsome, because it fills us with delight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And we speak of a “sullen sky.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not that there is any such feeling in the clouds, but because men are affected with sullenness at the sight of such an appearance of the skies, it is called sullen for this reason, that it makes us sullen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So also God is said to “know” when He causes us to know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God says to Abraham, “Now I know that thou fearest God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did He then not know it before then?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Abraham did not know himself till then: for it was in that very trial he came to know himself.…And God is said to “know” that which He had caused him to know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did Peter know himself, when he said to the Physician, “I will be with Thee even unto death?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Physician had felt his pulse, and knew what was going on within His patient’s soul: the patient knew it not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The crisis of trial came; and the Physician approved the correctness of His opinion: the sick man gave up his presumption.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus God at once “knows” it and “searches it out.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He knows it already.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why does He ‘search it out’?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thy sake: that thou mayest come to know thine own self, and mayest return thanks to Him that made thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Shall not God search it out?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For, for Thy sake we are killed all the day long: we are counted as sheep for the slaughter” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For you may see a man being put to death; you do not know why he is being put to death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God knoweth this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The thing in itself is hid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But some one will say to me, “See, he is detained in prison for the name of Christ, he is a confessor for the name of Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do not heretics also confess the name of Christ, and yet they do not die for His sake?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay more; let me say it, in the Catholic Church itself, do you think there either are, or have been wanting persons such as would suffer for the sake of glory among men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were there no such persons, the Apostle would not say, “Though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He knew therefore that there might be some persons, who did this not from “charity,” but out of vainglory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is therefore hid from us; God alone sees this; we cannot see it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He alone can judge of this, who “knoweth the secrets of the heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For,” for Thy sake “are we killed all the day long; we are counted as sheep for the slaughter.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have already mentioned that from hence the Apostle Paul had borrowed a text for the encouragement of the Martyrs: that they might not “faint in the tribulations” undergone by them for the name of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Awake; why sleepest Thou, O Lord?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is addressed, and who is the speaker?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would not he be more correctly said to sleep and slumber, who speaks such words as these?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He replies to you, I know what I am saying: I know that “He that keepeth Israel doth not sleep:” but yet the Martyrs cry, “Awake; why sleepest Thou, O Lord?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Lord Jesus, Thou wast slain; Thou didst “sleep” in Thy Passion; to us Thou hast now “awaked” from sleep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “we” know that Thou hast now “awaked” again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To what purpose hast Thou awaked and risen again?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Gentiles that persecute us, think Thee to be dead; do not believe Thee to have risen again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Arise Thou” then to them also!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Why sleepest Thou,” though not to us, yet to them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if they already believed Thee to have risen again, could they persecute us who believe in Thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why do they persecute?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Destroy, slay so and so, whoever have believed in Thee, such an one, who died an ill death!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As yet to them “Thou sleepest;” arise to them, that they may perceive that Thou hast “awaked” again; and may be at rest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, it has come to pass, while the Martyrs die, and say these things; while they sleep, and “awaken” Christ, truly dead in their sleepings, Christ has, in a certain sense, risen again in the Gentiles; i.e. it becomes believed, that He has risen again; so by degrees they themselves, becoming converted to Christ by believing, collected a numerous body: such as the persecutors dreaded; and the persecutions have come to an end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because Christ, who before was asleep to them, as not believing, hath risen in the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Arise, and cast us not off for ever!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Wherefore hidest Thou Thy face:” as if Thou wert not present; as if thou hadst forgotten us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And forgettest our misery and trouble?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For our soul is bowed down to the dust” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is it bowed down?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To the dust:” i.e. dust persecutes us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They persecute us, of whom Thou hast said, “The ungodly are not so; but are like the dust, which the wind driveth away from the face of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Our belly hath cleaved to the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He seems to me to have expressed the punishment of the extreme of humiliation, in which, when any one prostrates himself, “his belly cleaveth to the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whosoever is humbled so as to be on his knees, has yet a lower degree of humiliation to which he can come: but he who is so humbled, that his “belly cleaveth to the ground,” there is no farther humiliation for him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Should one wish to do still farther, it will, after that point, be not bowing him down, but crushing him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps then he may have meant this: We are “bowed down very low” in this dust; there is no farther point to which humiliation can go.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Humiliation has now reached its highest point: let mercy then come also.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Arise, O Lord, help us” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And indeed, dearly beloved, He has arisen and helped us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when he awaked (i.e. when He arose again, and became known to the Gentiles) on the cessation of persecutions, even those who had cleaved to the earth were raised up from the earth, and on performing penance, have been restored to Christ’s body, feeble and imperfect though they were: so that in them was fulfilled the text, “Thine eyes did see my substance yet being imperfect; and in Thy book shall they all be written.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Arise, O Lord, help us, and redeem us for Thy Name’s sake;” that is to say, freely; for Thy Name’s sake, not for the sake of my merits: because Thou hast vouchsafed to do it, not because I am worthy that Thou shouldest do it unto me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this very thing, that “we have not forgotten Thee;” that “our heart hath not gone back;” that we “have not stretched out our hands to any strange god;” how should we have been able to achieve, except with Thy help?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How should we have strength for it, except through Thy appealing to us within, exhorting us, and not forsaking us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whether then we suffer in tribulations, or rejoice in prosperities, redeem Thou us, not for our merits, but for Thy Name’s sake.
